skill.name: segment-cdp
skill.description: Expert patterns for Segment Customer Data Platform including Analytics.js, server-side tracking, tracking plans with Protocols, identity resolution, destinations configuration, and data governance best practices. Use when: segment, analytics.js, customer data platform, cdp, tracking plan.
skill.summary: Guidance for Segment CDP tracking, identity calls, tracking plans, and data governance patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported four high-severity weak cryptography matches, but all are false positives in Markdown guidance text. No executable code, cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, data exfiltration pattern, or hidden risk factor was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography match in description
low_findings.description: The static alert at SKILL.md:3 points to descriptive frontmatter text about Segment CDP. It does not contain cryptographic code, weak algorithms, secrets, or executable behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language metadata only. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher selection, or code execution path exists there.

low_findings.title: False positive duplicate weak cryptography match
low_findings.description: The duplicate static alert at SKILL.md:3 is another match against the same descriptive frontmatter line. It is safe because the line only summarizes Segment CDP topics.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The duplicated match has the same non-executable context as the first alert. There is no evidence of weak cryptography or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography match in section heading
low_findings.description: The static alert at SKILL.md:24 points to a tracking plan section heading. The heading is documentation about event schema design, not cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown heading and cannot perform cryptographic operations. The surrounding text discusses tracking plan structure only.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography match in guidance text
low_findings.description: The static alert at SKILL.md:26 points to guidance about event schema naming. It does not recommend or implement any weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain documentation about Object plus Action event names. No crypto library, algorithm name, or executable code is present.

content.value_statement: Teams often create inconsistent Segment events across browser and server sources. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward structured tracking plans, identity calls, and governance checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains Analytics.js browser tracking patterns for page, track, identify, and group calls.
Describes server-side Segment tracking with batching for backend events and webhooks.
Guides tracking plan design using Object plus Action event naming.
Highlights identity resolution order and the importance of identify before track.
Calls out common anti-patterns in Segment event and property modeling.
limitations: Does not include executable integration code or installation scripts.
Does not validate a live Segment workspace or destination configuration.
Does not provide complete Protocols rule definitions.
Uses brief guidance and should be paired with official Segment documentation.

use_cases.title: Plan product analytics events
use_cases.description: Create consistent event names, required properties, and validation expectations before engineers implement tracking.

use_cases.title: Review tracking implementation choices
use_cases.description: Compare browser tracking and server-side tracking for sensitive backend events, webhooks, and reliability needs.

use_cases.title: Improve data governance
use_cases.description: Identify dynamic event names, missing identify calls, and property modeling issues before they reach destinations.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a basic tracking plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me draft a Segment tracking plan for these product events: [events]. Include event names, required properties, and validation notes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Analytics.js usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this browser tracking approach for Segment Analytics.js. Check page, track, identify, and group usage for sequencing issues.

prompt_templates.title: Choose browser or server tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare browser and server-side Segment tracking for this workflow: [workflow]. Recommend where each event should be sent from.

prompt_templates.title: Audit event governance risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Segment event model for governance risks. Look for dynamic event names, property misuse, identity gaps, and Protocols enforcement needs.

output_examples.input: Plan tracking for account creation and first project setup.
output_examples.output: A structured tracking plan with two Object plus Action event names, required user identifiers, core properties, and validation notes.

output_examples.input: Decide whether checkout events should be browser or server-side.
output_examples.output: A source recommendation that places sensitive payment-adjacent events on the server and keeps page interaction events in the browser.

output_examples.input: Review a Segment model with many dynamic event names.
output_examples.output: A governance review that consolidates event names, moves variable details into properties, and flags Protocols rules to add.

best_practices: Define event names and required properties before implementation starts.
Call identify before track when events depend on a known user.
Use server-side tracking for backend events, webhooks, and sensitive data flows.
anti_patterns: Creating dynamic event names from user actions or product states.
Representing properties as separate events instead of event attributes.
Sending tracked events before identity context is available.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan and review Segment CDP tracking patterns for browser events, server events, identity calls, and governance.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Segment?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance only and does not access Segment workspaces, APIs, or destinations.

faq.question: Can it generate a complete tracking plan?
faq.answer: It can draft tracking plan structure and validation notes. Teams should review details against product and data requirements.

faq.question: Does it support server-side tracking decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when backend events, webhooks, and sensitive flows should use server-side Segment tracking.

faq.question: Can it detect bad event naming patterns?
faq.answer: It can flag common modeling issues such as dynamic event names and tracking properties as separate events.

faq.question: Should I still use official Segment documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Use this skill for workflow guidance and official Segment documentation for current platform details.
